action.reasoning: Observation: Best test score is 0.6199 from DINOv2 5-epoch full fine-tuning. All other approaches (more epochs, frozen, CLIP, partial fine-tuning) are worse. Analysis: The sweet spot is minimal fine-tuning. The weak classes are squirrel (0.009-0.139), bobcat (0.175-0.456), bird (0.296-0.685). Strategy: Go back to original approach but improve it with: (1) label smoothing to prevent overconfidence, (2) mixup augmentation for better generalization, (3) use both CLS + mean patch tokens, (4) more aggressive TTA in predict.py, (5) train exactly 5 epochs. Also try a completely different angle: use the model's uncertainty to boost rare class predictions.
